question.text: Scenario: Arun Kumar is analyzing Reliance Industries (RIL) stock using multiple technical indicators. Current observations: (1) RIL CMP: Rs.2,850. (2) 50-day SMA: Rs.2,780 (rising). 200-day SMA: Rs.2,650 (rising). (3) The 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA one month ago. (4) RSI: 58. (5) Bollinger Bands: Upper band Rs.2,950, Middle band Rs.2,780, Lower band Rs.2,610. Price is between middle and upper band. (6) Strong support at Rs.2,600 (tested 3 times in last 6 months). (7) Resistance at Rs.2,950 (tested twice). (8) Volume has been above average on recent up-days. (9) MACD is above the signal line and above the zero baseline. Question 5 of 5: Based on all technical indicators, Arun's recommendation for RIL should be:

answer.text: Strong Sell because the stock is at Rs.2,850 — too high
Buy/Hold with a target near resistance at Rs.2,950 and stop-loss near the 50-day SMA at Rs.2,780 — all indicators are bullish
Wait indefinitely for the stock to fall to Rs.2,000
Short sell immediately with no stop-loss

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All indicators are bullish: Golden Cross confirmed, Price > MAs, RSI neutral (58), MACD bullish, above-average volume on up-days. Target = Rs.2,950 (resistance), Stop-loss = Rs.2,780 (50 SMA). **The Trap:** Price being high doesn't mean sell — trend analysis matters more. Never trade without a stop-loss. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set target at Rs.2,950 resistance. If it breaks through on volume, trail stop-loss and target Rs.3,100-3,200 next.
